assumptions: The developer has a supported browser, email address, and a workstation that can install Node.js LTS and npm.
No Atlassian account, Forge API token, cloud developer site, or Forge app exists yet.

developer_goal: Create an Atlassian developer identity and reach a first visible Forge hello-world issue panel on Jira Cloud.

first_success.outcome: Installed Forge app panel visible on a Jira issue.
first_success.observable_signal: On a signed-in Jira issue, selecting Apps then the installed app shows the hello-world issue panel.

route_selection.surface: Forge CLI with free cloud developer site and Jira hello-world issue panel
route_selection.rule: Complete shared Forge setup, then select one host-product hello world. Prefer the vendor's recommended or featured first-app route when peer product tutorials remain otherwise comparable.
route_selection.selected: Forge CLI Jira hello-world issue panel

candidates.name: Forge CLI Jira hello-world issue panel
candidates.reason: Start building with Atlassian and Jira Software Getting started with Forge both feature the Jira hello-world tutorial after recommending Forge. Shared getting started still lists peers, but the featured first-app links resolve the tie to Jira.

candidates.name: Forge CLI Confluence hello world
candidates.reason: Peer official tutorial on the same cloud developer site with a different module and verification surface.

candidates.name: Forge CLI Bitbucket hello world
candidates.reason: Peer official tutorial that requires a shared Bitbucket team workspace rather than the free cloud developer site path.

candidates.name: Forge CLI Jira Service Management hello world
candidates.reason: Peer official tutorial with a different queue-page module and verification navigation.

candidates.name: Raw Jira REST without Forge
candidates.reason: Atlassian recommends Forge for new cloud apps; REST alone is not the documented first-app onboarding path.

required_path.action: Create or sign in to an Atlassian account usable for Forge development.

required_fields.label: Work email or social identity
required_fields.notes: Cloud developer signup shows work email plus Google, Microsoft, Apple, and Slack. Exact password and email-verification fields for a cold email route remain unverified.

required_path.observable_result: The developer has an Atlassian account email usable for API token and cloud developer site creation.

required_path.action: Install Node.js LTS 22.x or 24.x and verify with `node --version`.
Install the Forge CLI globally with `npm install -g @forge/cli` and verify with `forge --version`.
required_path.observable_result: The Forge CLI prints its version.

required_path.action: Create an Atlassian API token with scopes for Forge.

required_fields.label: Forge scopes
required_fields.notes: Confirm recommended scopes or select a custom subset.

required_path.observable_result: The full Forge-scoped token value is available to copy for CLI login.

required_path.action: Run `forge login` and authenticate with the Atlassian account email and scoped token.
Create the free Atlassian cloud developer site and complete its setup wizard.
required_path.observable_result: A free cloud developer site with Jira available is ready for app install.

required_path.action: Run `forge create`, choose `development`, select Jira UI Kit `jira-issue-panel`, and enter the generated app directory.
required_path.observable_result: The generated app directory contains the manifest and source files.

required_path.action: Run `forge deploy` from the app top-level directory.
required_path.observable_result: Forge reports a successful deploy without compilation errors.

required_path.action: Run `forge install`, select Jira, enter the developer-site URL, and complete any prompted consent.

required_fields.label: Install consent
required_fields.notes: Complete any prompted authorization for required API access.

required_path.observable_result: The CLI displays the successful installation message.

required_path.action: While signed in, open a Jira issue, select Apps, and select the installed app.
required_path.observable_result: The hello-world issue panel is visible on the Jira issue.

prerequisites.description: JavaScript familiarity is expected by the Forge getting-started guide.
Forge apps cannot be viewed by anonymous users; the tester must be logged in to the cloud developer site.

external_gates.description: A scoped API token with Forge selected must be created in Atlassian account security settings before CLI login.
forge install requires authorizing the app onto the chosen Jira site context.

unavoidable_waits.description: Atlassian must finish provisioning the free cloud developer site before install and verification.
forge deploy must finish building and deploying before forge install can link the app to the site.

platform_outcomes.description: Forge CLI stores authenticated developer credentials after forge login or environment-variable auth.
forge deploy builds and deploys the app; forge install links it to the developer site.

excluded.item: Optional manifest title customization before first deploy
excluded.reason: The tutorial changes the panel title, but a default title still reaches a visible hello-world panel.
Used for iterative local changes after first install, not required to observe first success.

excluded.item: Part 2 Jira API call and Part 3 UI Kit tutorials
excluded.reason: Occur after the Part 1 hello-world panel is visible.
Occur after local install and hello-world verification.
Different product surface from the shared Forge setup and selected Jira hello world.

sources.title: Prepare to build your first Forge app
evidence_supported: Node.js 22.x or 24.x and Forge CLI setup

sources.title: Build a Jira hello world app
Atlassian cloud developer site signup
evidence_supported: Free development sites for Jira Software, Jira Service Management, and Confluence
Work email and social signup options

sources.title: Start building with Atlassian
evidence_supported: Jira hello world featured as a getting-started tutorial

sources.title: Getting started with Forge for Jira Software
evidence_supported: Building a hello world app in Jira listed first under Start today

uncertainties.question: What exact required fields appear after submitting work email on a cold Atlassian account signup, including password and email verification?
uncertainties.impact: Account-creation field counts cannot be finalized, so public action and field counts stay withheld.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated cold walkthrough of Atlassian account creation through the email route without an existing login.
